text: Delivered Svds.svd (gale.spectral): dense DMat + rectangular operator entries, GKL bidiagonalization (CGS-twice both sequences, growing subspace, taller-side orientation with transposeOp + U/V swap-back), projected solve via Jordan-Wielandt augmented tridiagonal per parity doc §3 (NOT normal equations). Adversarial review: math hand-verified correct (interleave, unpacking, signs, both orientations); review follow-ups applied (kernel-exhaustion Left carve-out doc, rank-semantics honesty in SVD.scala, wide matrix-free operator test). THE NEW TEST FOUND A REAL LATENT BUG: exact rank deficiency (alpha-breakdown with pending right vector) truncated the projected bidiagonal square, dropping the trailing beta -> wrong matrix, zero convergence, growth disabled. Fixed by lead: rectangular mEff x (mEff+1) projected problem (odd-dim augmented tridiagonal keeps beta), Ritz decode extended to vCount=mEff+1 right coefficients; exact sigma recovered to 1e-15 on both orientations; regression tests added. Verified: coreJVM 233/233, coreJS 233/233, laws 17/17 both, parityTest 25/25. Committed. Known limitations documented: absolute (not relative) accuracy ~eps*normA for smallest sigma; no shift-target modes; one-sided operators throw from the primitive layer.
